macro_summary: Stocks edged higher on Friday with the S&P 500 at 720.65 (+0.11%) and Nasdaq outperforming (+0.94%) as AI demand and easing Hormuz fears supported semis. WTI slipped to $101.81 (-0.13%) after Trump proposed US naval escort through the Strait of Hormuz. The Fed held rates steady at 3.50–3.75%, citing inflation uncertainty, pushing rate-cut expectations to late 2026.
commodities_note: WTI eased to $101.81 as Hormuz supply fears cooled; gold pulled back to $4,610 (-0.74%). The US10Y remained elevated at 4.38%, keeping pressure on Singapore bank NIMs and growth-stock multiples.

singapore.summary: Singapore cluster remains bifurcated: DBS extended with RSI 71 and NIM compression, OCBC and UOB on HOLD ahead of earnings this week. MAS kept SGD appreciation pace, and US10Y at 4.38% keeps dual NIM headwinds intact. OCBC (earnings May 8) could break the stalemate if NIM stabilizes above 4.2%.
singapore.key_development: Earnings week for OCBC and UOB is the next catalyst; DBS remains hard-blocked on RSI.

semiconductors.summary: Semis cluster remains heavily extended, with seven of eight names on CAUTION and ASML the lone WATCH. SOXX at 465.75 (+1.73%) masks divergence: AMD, INTC, MU, LITE all above +20% SMA50, while ASML at +2% is the only name within striking distance of an entry. Earnings this week (AMD Wed, LITE Wed) are binary risks that could reset technicals or extend extremes.
semiconductors.key_development: AMD and LITE report Wednesday; historically AMD moves down post-earnings and LITE moves up, creating an asymmetric binary.

big_tech.summary: BigTech cluster mostly blocked: AMZN and GOOG extreme extension, PLTR faces earnings tonight. MSFT is the only actionable name, upgraded to WATCH after Azure revenue +40% and OpenAI partnership restructuring. Tonight's PLTR print and Friday's jobs report are the next cluster catalysts.
big_tech.key_development: MSFT's Azure strength and OpenAI revenue cap restructuring improve the risk/reward, but price must pull back to become actionable.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: NVIDIA $2B photonics investment partner; co-packaged optics for AI datacenters — LITE is the watchlist name, but COHR benefits equally.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR pulls back 10% from current levels; photonics is a multi-year AI interconnect theme.

interconnected.name: SK Hynix
interconnected.reason: Supplies HBM memory to NVDA and AMD; capacity expansion signals tight market and pricing power.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if 000660.KS pulls back 8-10% from current levels; monitor MU earnings for pricing confirmation.

active_risks: Iran-Hormuz crisis: Strait disrupted since March, US now 'guiding' ships; WTI at $101.81 off crisis highs.
US-China tech trade war: sanctions on COMAC, port fees, and semiconductor export controls fuel Chinese innovation.
Fed rate-hold with inflation uncertainty; US10Y at 4.38% keeps pressure on growth stocks and Singapore bank NIMs.
geopolitical.market_impact: Elevated oil supports defense and logistics names; semis remain extended despite easing supply fears. Singapore banks' NIM compression persists with US10Y above 4.20% and strong SGD. ASML and MSFT are the only watchlist names with workable entries.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Trump announced US will 'guide' ships through Hormuz, easing blockade fears but no reopening yet.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Favor select entry on ASML and WRD; avoid extended semis and pre-earnings binaries. Hold off on Singapore banks until NIM clarity.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Cautiously bullish with pockets of opportunity
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: Hormuz easing reduces oil shock tail risk, but extension across semis and NIM compression in Singapore keep entries limited — ASML and WRD offer the most actionable setups.
